markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Use MemoryOntology/schema matches to narrow candidate facts and reduce off-topic graph expansion. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: SchemaFirstFiltering leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SchemaFirstFiltering is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91b6df48; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: Schema scope applied post-hoc over a capped unordered fact load (recall gap); never invoked by the planner before widening
evidence.fix: Scope predicate pushed into the fact SQL (list_memory_facts_in_schema_scope, ANY(scope)); schema_first_filter routes through it when scope resolves; the executed pipeline (executor.rs) invokes schema_first_filter before graph widening
evidence.tests: knowledge_retrieval_bridges_tests 3 passed incl. recall-gap proof (in-scope fact buried under 5 newer off-topic facts, cap 3, still returned)

findings.title: schema scope is applied post-hoc over a capped, unordered fact load (recall gap)
findings.evidence: schema_first_filter (schema_filter.rs:138-152) loads up to fact_limit facts via list_memory_facts then filters in memory by in-scope terms; the scope is NOT pushed into SQL. For a large fact base the in-scope facts can fall outside the first fact_limit rows and be silently missed.
findings.fix: Push the resolved term scope into the SQL (WHERE predicate_term_id IN (...) OR object_entity_id IN (...)) so the limit bounds in-scope facts, not an arbitrary prefix.

findings.title: schema-first filtering is not invoked by the planner before widening
findings.evidence: CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner (planner.rs) never calls schema_first_filter; the graph/hybrid widen (steps 3 and 5) run without narrowing to schema scope. The primitive is unit-tested but unwired, so the spec use-schema-first-before-widening is unrealized in the plan path.
findings.fix: Invoke schema_first_filter to seed graph_neighborhood / candidate sets before the planner emits a graph/hybrid route.

missing_tests: test: planner narrows graph seeds via schema scope before widening

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-131 SchemaFirstFiltering requires using MemoryOntology/schema matches to narrow candidate facts before graph/hybrid widening, PG-backed, invoked on the executed retrieval path. The code delivers this with a real sqlx scope pushdown over knowledge_memory_facts, wired into the live executor before graph traversal, and proven by a managed-PostgreSQL recall-gap test. Both prior adversarial-v2 MEDIUM findings (post-hoc recall gap; unwired filter) are genuinely fixed.
findings: schema_filter.rs:168-196 schema_first_filter resolves query tokens to ontology terms (resolve_query_schema) then, when scope resolves, routes through the SQL pushdown list_memory_facts_in_schema_scope; empty scope keeps recent facts as advisory and signals caller-must-widen via has_schema_scope()==false. Correct narrow-before-widen semantics.

knowledge_memory.rs:774-796 list_memory_facts_in_schema_scope is a real sqlx PG query: WHERE workspace_id=$1 AND (predicate_term_id = ANY($2) OR object_entity_id = ANY($2)) ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $3. Scope predicate is pushed into SQL so the row cap bounds in-scope facts, not an arbitrary newest-N prefix. This is the exact fix for prior adversarial finding #1 (post-hoc recall gap).

executor.rs:110-138 the EXECUTED retrieval pipeline calls schema_first_filter before graph traversal, seeds GraphTraversalPlanner from schema.seed_entity_ids(), and narrows traversal via schema_scoped_graph_policy(graph_policy, &schema.edge_type_allowlist). This wires the filter into the live plan path, fixing prior adversarial finding #2 (filter was unit-tested but never invoked by the planner).

tests/knowledge_retrieval_bridges_tests.rs:44-177 schema_first_filter_narrows_to_query_scope runs on real PostgreSQL (skip_if_no_pg!, MemoryFixture::setup, pool_for over isolated schema). It creates a real ontology term+alias, an in-scope fact and an off-topic fact, then buries the in-scope fact under 5 NEWER off-topic facts with cap=3 and proves the in-scope fact is still returned (candidate_facts.len()==1). This is genuine runtime proof of the recall-gap fix against managed PG, not a mock.

tests/knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:1-9,43-152 fixture is managed-PG auto-discovery with full migration chain, documented 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks.' Columns predicate_term_id/object_entity_id are real, defined in migration 0241_knowledge_memory_facts.sql:45. Sub-rule 2 satisfied.

Sub-rule 1: grep of schema_filter.rs, executor.rs, knowledge_memory.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/stub/placeholder/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' returned zero hits on the MT-131 path.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec (v02.194) 02-system-architecture.md:1516 'Graph queries narrow candidate sets before vector search' and section 2.3.14.1.4 (rich metadata enables filtering) directly support the schema-first narrow-before-widen behavior MT-131 implements.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): the lifecycle.evidence note mentions 'migration 0260' for the original MT-131 commit; the recall-gap hardening (e5180a83) correctly required no new migration since it queries existing 0241 columns. Not a code defect.
evidence_checked: packet.json allowed/forbidden paths (read via task brief)
grep for deferred-live escapes across MT-131 source paths (zero hits)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
